_meta.description: Organic fertilizer mixing guide — Alex's 5-tea system

teas.cow_manure_tea: Balanced N + organic matter + microbes. Gentle, safe for seedlings.
teas.chicken_manure_tea: High N + P. Powerful vegetative booster. Use sparingly — can burn.
teas.plant_based_tea: NPK + micronutrients + mild pest suppression. Safe for all stages.
teas.wood_ash_tea: K + Ca + Mg. Raises pH. Flowering/fruiting booster. Avoid for potatoes and leafy greens.
teas.yeast_fertilizer: Microbial stimulant. B-vitamins + root growth hormones. Keep doses small.

_meta.yeast_preparation: Dissolve 1 packet (10g) baker's yeast + 1-2 tbsp sugar in 1L lukewarm water (~30C). Ferment 12-24h until bubbling. Use within 24h of activation.

yeast_dosing.foliar_spray: 1 tsp per 1 litre of final diluted mix
yeast_dosing.soil_drench: 1 tbsp per 5 litres of final diluted mix

_meta.thyme_oil_mosquito_control: Add 2-3 drops thyme oil per litre of stored tea to deter mosquito breeding. Add after brewing, just before application.
application_tips: Foliar spray: early morning or late afternoon only — never midday sun
Drench: apply at root zone, avoid stem contact
Never mix teas with fungicides or pesticides — apply 3-5 days apart
Never mix wood ash tea with chicken manure tea in the same application
Stir teas daily to maintain aerobic conditions and prevent bad fermentation
Use teas within 3-5 days of brewing for best microbial activity
Always dilute before applying — never apply concentrate directly to plants
_meta.dilution_note: final_dilution is TEA CONCENTRATE parts : WATER parts. e.g. 25 means 1 cup tea per 25 cups water. mix_parts shows relative proportions of each tea concentrate to combine BEFORE final dilution with water.

seedling.description: From 2-3 true leaves until transplant (~3-6 weeks)

foliar.mixing_example: Per 1L spray bottle: 20 ml cow manure tea, 20 ml plant-based tea, top up to 1L with water (~960ml water)
foliar.note: Very mild - leafy greens burn easily.

drench.mixing_example: Per 5L watering can: 125 ml cow manure tea, 125 ml plant-based tea, top up to 5L with water (~4750ml water)
drench.note: Cow + plant + yeast. Drench every 7-10 days.

mid_vegetative.description: Active stem and leaf growth, canopy forming

foliar.mixing_example: Per 1L spray bottle: 17 ml cow manure tea, 17 ml chicken manure tea, 17 ml plant-based tea, top up to 1L with water (~949ml water)
foliar.note: Nitrogen drives leaf mass. No wood ash for leafy greens.

drench.mixing_example: Per 5L watering can: 111 ml cow manure tea, 111 ml chicken manure tea, 111 ml plant-based tea, top up to 5L with water (~4667ml water)
drench.note: Chicken tea drench every 10 days for lush growth.

flowering.description: Buds forming through open flowers / head formation

foliar.mixing_example: Per 1L spray bottle: 20 ml cow manure tea, 20 ml plant-based tea, top up to 1L with water (~960ml water)
foliar.note: Maintain leaf quality. Avoid bolting by not over-feeding nitrogen.

drench.mixing_example: Per 5L watering can: 167 ml cow manure tea, 167 ml plant-based tea, top up to 5L with water (~4666ml water)
